International football competition

The **2005 [South American Youth Championship](/wiki/South_American_Youth_Football_Championship "South American Youth Football Championship")** (Sudamericana sub-20) was a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") competition contested by all ten U-20 national football teams of [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL"). The tournament was held in [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") between 13 January and 6 February 2005, it was the 22nd time the competition has been held and the third to take place in Colombia. Colombia finished undefeated, winning their second trophy.

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2005_South_American_U-20_Championship&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

The teams are separated in two groups of five, and each team plays four matches in a pure [round-robin](/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament") stage. The three top competitors advance to a single final group of six, wherein each team plays five matches. The top four teams in the final group qualify to the [2005 FIFA World Youth Championship](/wiki/2005_FIFA_World_Youth_Championship "2005 FIFA World Youth Championship")
